title: Automated Pill Dispenser
description: RTC-scheduled rotating carousel dispensing medication at programmed times. An NEMA17 stepper rotates a 7-compartment drum; a load-cell confirms pill ejection; a buzzer and OLED alert the user. Missed doses are logged to Serial.
objective: Dispense up to 4 doses/day on a programmable schedule; confirm dispensing via weight change ≥ 0.3 g; alert user with buzzer for ≥ 60 s if dose not removed; log missed doses.
constraints: 5 V USB power (1 A); ≤ £30 BOM; single Arduino Nano; 7-compartment carousel; I²C peripherals only.
supporting_fact_titles: I²C Real-Time Clock — DS3231 Timekeeping and Alarms
Stepper Motor Drive — Full Step and Microstepping
OLED SSD1306 Monochrome Display I²C Interface

element_usage_notes.DS3231 RTC Alarm Scheduling Module: Up to 4 alarms per day programmed via serial menu at first run; stored in DS3231 alarm registers + AT24C32 EEPROM on the same module.
element_usage_notes.NEMA17 Stepper DRV8825 Microstepping Dri: Full-step mode (200 steps/rev); carousel 360° / 7 compartments = 51.4°/step; ~114 steps per position using AccelStepper ramp.
element_usage_notes.SSD1306 OLED Display UI Module: Shows next dose time, current compartment number, and last dispensed timestamp. 2 s refresh.
element_usage_notes.Arduino Uno/Nano MCU Platform: Nano 3.0; DRV8825 STEP/DIR on D8/D9; DS3231 INT on D3 (INT1); buzzer on D7; load-cell HX711 CLK on D4, DOUT on D5.

element_interactions.data: INT falling edge (hardware interrupt on D3)
element_interactions.description: RTC fires alarm at each programmed dose time. ISR sets dispensing_requested flag; main loop advances carousel one compartment position and enables buzzer.
AccelStepper ramps to 200 step/s to advance carousel by one 51.4° compartment. End-of-travel homing on startup via limit switch on compartment 1.
OLED updated after each dispense event and every 2 s during idle to show countdown to next dose.
